text: Amarienborg
Amarienborg
Amarienborg
Amarienborg is the home of the Danish royal family. The highlight of a visit is the change of guard at 12:00.

Frederiks Kirke
Frederiks Kirke
Frederiks Kirke
Frederiks Kirke, also known as Marble Church, is known for its rococo architecture, not modern architecture. It's one of the most iconic buildings in Copenhagen.

Rundetaarn
Rundetaarn
Rundetaarn
Rundetaarn is a 17th-century tower built as an astronomical observatory. It offers a great view of Copenhagen.

Christiansborg Palace
Christiansborg Palace
Christiansborg Palace
Christiansborg Palace is one of the most significant landmarks in Copenhagen, both historically and politically. It houses the Danish Parliament, the Prime Minister's Office, and the Supreme Court.
